tree.node:
I always end up wandering to this place…
Continue

You used to drag me here every day, said that the gods|listened to our prayers better at sunrise.
Continue

I almost wanted to believe you at some point,|you’re always right after all.
Continue

But if they really listened to my prayers…|Why are you not coming back to me?
Continue

Damn it, why would you want to go to the castle?|No one comes back from there anymore, not even you…
Continue

I don’t know what I’ll find inside...|but I can’t stand here waiting anymore.
Continue

Nia, I’m coming to find you.
Continue

...Or whatever remains of you.
Continue

Maybe I should ask the Gravekeeper where to go,|she’s been here for longer than anyone.
Continue

If there’s a way into the castle, she’ll know.
Continue

You have been pacing around|the cemetery for quite a while.
Continue

People who wander around like this are either lost…|or afraid of where they need to go next.
Continue

I wonder which one you are.
I'm looking for a way into the castle.
Someone I care about is lost.

The castle... Many people|go there looking for answers.
Continue

But they end up here instead,|keeping me company.
Continue

The main gates are closed and watched,|but there is an older entrance.
Continue

Just behind the castle, lies a forgotten elevator|that goes right into the castle dungeons.
Continue

If you truly desire to go below,|you should not go empty-handed.
Continue

There is a rusty farming tool laying nearby.
Continue

It may help you defend yourself…|or at least buy you some time.
Thank you.
Do you really stand here all day?

I cannot promise that you will find what|you are looking for.
Continue

But maybe if you descend low enough, the secrets of|the castle will be unveiled to you.
Continue

Before going down, however, you may|wish to stop by the statue again.
Continue

It shall be the last peaceful moment|you will have for quite a while.
End Conversation

Someone must do so.
Continue

The dead are patient,|but they do not bury themselves.
Continue

On top of that, they have been|increasingly restless lately.
Continue

Whatever is happening behind those|castle walls, it is complete blasphemy.
Continue

Which is why, before going down, you may|wish to stop by the statue again.
Continue

It shall be the last peaceful moment|you will have for quite a while.
End Conversation

My deepest condolences, young one.
Continue

Is it perhaps the lovely damsel that|frequented the castle?
Continue

She seemed like a resourceful one,|it is difficult to believe that she|went there for no reason at all.
Continue

However, just behind the castle, there’s|a forgotten elevator that goes|right into the castle dungeons.
Continue

If you truly desire to go below,|you should not go empty-handed.
Continue

There is a rusty farming tool laying nearby.
Continue

It may help you defend yourself…|or at least buy you some time.
Thank you
What's happening to the dead?

I cannot promise that you will find|what you are looking for.
Continue

But maybe if you descend low enough,|the secrets of the castle will be unveiled to you.
Continue

Before going down, however, you may|wish to stop by the statue again.
Continue

It shall be the last peaceful moment|you will have for quite a while.
End conversation

They do not rest in peace anymore.
Continue

Soulless carcasses are meant to return to|the Earth that birthed them.
Continue

That is the natural order.
Continue

However, something behind those castle|walls is disturbing that.
Continue

Which is why, before going down, you may wish|to stop by the statue again.
Continue

It shall be the last peaceful moment|you will have for quite a while.
End conversation

I see that you’re finally heading out,|it must take a lot of courage.
I need to find her.
Do you have anything that could help me?

I understand, Nia’s disappearance is|felt throughout the entire Refuge.
Continue

Be careful Garnik, I have no idea|what you may find out there.
End conversation

I… maybe I do have something.
Continue

I’ve been holding onto this for quite|some time, it’s a little something to|fight back the infected air.
Continue

Take it, I’ve got no clue what’s inside|the castle… but hundreds of corpses|are walking out from those doors.
Wait, won’t you need this?
I changed my mind, please keep it

Haha… it’s okay, you’ll need it|more than I do.
Continue

I don’t have much left,|nor much to fight for.
Continue

So I’ll try my best to help|with what little I have left.
End conversation

That's okay, you can come to me|whenever you need anything.
End conversation

... What?
Are you okay?

I... lost my dog.
Continue

Her name is Barbara... she's my only friend and I can't find her anywhere...
I'll tell you if I find her.

Wait! You found her??
Would you also like to come?

I trust her... and if she trusts you too, I guess I can come along.
End conversation

The gods are watching you. |You feel a fleeting sensation of peace.
Pray and save

Woof, woof! (she seems to want something to play with)
I'll go look for something.

Woof! Arf arf!! (she seems happy!)
Come with me!

dialogs.tree:
isRepeatable="true">
The lever has been pulled, you hear a gate opening.
Continue

tree.node:
Hello, welcome to my shop.
Continue

L-locked? There has to be a way to open it...
Continue

…Garnik.
Continue

You actually made it. I wasn't sure if you would.
Nia! Are you hurt?

I'm fine. I've been fine this whole time.
Then why didn't you say anything?!

Because you would've stopped me. And you know that I couldn't possibly say no to you.
I thought the king took you…

I came here willingly, Garnik.
…What?

He came to me. Offered me a laboratory, resources… a purpose.
Continue

So I said yes. No one dragged me here.
You switched sides… for a lab?

You don't understand, Garnik!|For the first time in my life,|someone looked at me and saw me as more than just a farmer.
Continue

He gave me the tools to actually do something with my mind and my capabilities.
…People are dead, Nia.

I-I know… but this infection was never meant to reach civilians.
Continue

All that was the king's decision. I built something and he chose how to deploy it.
You're lying. You knew.

N-no… I thought that it would end the war faster.
Continue

…So that you wouldn't need to suffer anymore.
Come home, Nia.

That's not my home anymore. I can't go back to who I was before this.
Continue

You wouldn't look at me with the same eyes.|No one would accept me if they knew what I had done.
I'll protect you.

…No.
Continue

I think you should stop caring about me.
I'm not leaving you alone.

Haha… you were always stubborn like that…
Continue

Fine. Then I suppose we have a problem.
Continue

You're… strong…
Continue

You grew up so much while I wasn't there… and I missed it all.
Don't give me that…

I mean it. The scrawny kid that struggled to even carry a hoe… he's gone.
Continue

I guess you don't need me anymore after all.
Continue

I won't fight anymore. Whatever you decide, I won't fight it.
Help me end this.
I can't forgive you.

…You want me to fix what I broke?
No. I want you back.

…Maybe she's not there anymore. The Nia you knew.
Continue

I'm a shell of who I once was… I don't deserve it.
Probably not…

…So?
But you can have a future.

What do you mean? After all that I've done, I'm tied to this lab.
Continue

There's nothing for me outside of here.
There is. I'll be with you.

Why?
You've always been by my side.

…
So don't think I'll ever leave yours.

…I know.
You're not going to object?

I can't. I don't have the right to.
Continue

I wish I could tell you that this wasn't worth it,|but I can no longer lie to you.
I don't know you anymore.

I could say the same thing.
Continue

It's okay. I'm not afraid.
Continue

…Is the farm still standing?
Yes, it is.

…Was the potato harvest good this year?
It was okay.

…Is everyone back at the refuge still hanging on?
Somewhat.

…Will you remember me|even after all of this?
…Goodbye, Nia.

You know, I'm also interested in going down that dungeon.
Why's that?

This horrible illness... these disgusting creatures...
Continue

They all must be coming out of somewhere.
Continue

And everything points to those dungeons.
Continue

Call it morbid curiosity, but I want to see what's down there.
Continue

The evils of this world... intrigue me.
Continue

However... I'm a bit wounded.
Continue

Some bandages will put me in good shape,|and I'll be able to come with you.
End conversation

That's it, thank you.
Would you like to come with me?

Yes, I will support you as much as I can.
Continue

Now, let's find what hideous truth lies down there...
End conversation
